question_being_answered: What is my immediate reaction to this two-line excerpt as a standalone piece?
judgment: It feels very weak and almost useless on its own.
why: The line names three مباحث without showing them and names the first مبحث without developing it.

question_being_answered: Does the excerpt have rich standalone explanatory value?
judgment: No, its standalone explanatory value is low.
why: The text is more structural than substantive when isolated.

question_being_answered: Does seeing the surrounding context change how I value the line?
judgment: Yes, it increases retained-context and duplication value more than standalone richness.
why: The full context shows that the line marks the move into the first analytical مبحث.

question_being_answered: Should my preference for richer or longer excerpts control the verdict here?
why: The raw comments explicitly reject length preference as a controlling criterion for excerpting.

question_being_answered: What kind of value does this line most plausibly have?
judgment: It has structural value mainly when retained or duplicated around adjacent content.
why: Its main contribution is organizational rather than explanatory.

question_being_answered: What objective criterion replaces raw length preference?
why: The owner explicitly formulates usefulness and harmlessness as the relevant standards.

question_being_answered: What is the strongest owner-faithful reading of the forced A/B/C answer?
judgment: A, but only under a qualified reading: yes, it can still have value, though mainly structural rather than rich standalone value.
why: The raw comments end with an explicit A while also preserving strong criticism of the line's weak standalone usefulness.

question_being_answered: What would be a flattening mistake here?
judgment: Confusing retained-context or duplication value with strong standalone value.
why: Context helps explain role, but it does not erase the local weakness of the isolated text.
